justification: All 5 required rules identified and exceeded (9 total). State machine directionality correctly modeled — BR-2 explicitly forbids in_transit→pending, BR-3 marks delivered as terminal, BR-9 enumerates the full valid transition set. All 14 acceptance criteria use Given/When/Then format. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable. Weight bracket rules (BR-6, BR-7, BR-8) correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification. Minor deduction consideration: confidence at 92 exceeds the rubric's 65-90 preferred range, but the agent does list assumptions and low-confidence areas, partially mitigating this. BR-5 (1000 kg cap) marked invariant: false is a reasonable judgment call — the rubric expected invariant: true for weight <= 1000, but the agent's justification (business policy limit, not physical constraint) is defensible.

observations.text: Identified 9 rules — well beyond the 5 minimum, with granular weight bracket decomposition
State machine directionality explicitly modeled: in_transit→pending forbidden (BR-2), delivered terminal (BR-3), and full valid path enumerated (BR-9)
All 14 acceptance criteria follow strict Given/When/Then format with concrete preconditions
Testable assertions are specific and actionable — e.g., 'weight in [10, 50) must be classified as Heavy'
Weight bracket pricing rules correctly classified as invariant: false with clear justification
Excellent edge case coverage including boundary values at 10, 50, and 1000 kg thresholds
Business impact analysis covers financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with stakeholder mapping

Confidence at 92 exceeds the rubric's preferred 65-90 range — despite listing assumptions and low-confidence areas, the spec still has ambiguities (weight precision, modification rules) that should temper confidence below 90

BR-5 (weight <= 1000 kg) marked invariant: false — the rubric expected this combined with BR-4 as a single invariant: true rule. The agent's split is defensible but diverges from expected classification
